title: PIR #2502803 - Pregnancy/Parenting PEIMS Data Analysis (2016-20)
content:
## PIR #2502803 Analysis Note

**Dataset:** District-level PEIMS data for pregnant and single-parent CTE students, Texas, 2016-17 through 2019-20
**Total rows:** 4,374 across 4 CSV files
**Unique districts:** 1,117

### Key Findings

**Pregnant CTE students (non-masked sum):**
- 2016-17: 703 | 2017-18: 664 | 2018-19: 585 | 2019-20: 471
- Declining trend over the 4-year period

**Single-parent CTE students (non-masked sum):**
- 2016-17: 1,417 | 2017-18: 1,276 | 2018-19: 1,134 | 2019-20: 763
- Similar declining trend, steeper in 2019-20

**Eligible pregnancy-related services days:**
- 2016-17: 451,884 | 2017-18: 382,624 | 2018-19: 351,300 | 2019-20: 308,280

**FERPA masking:** 953 masked pregnant values, 1,071 masked single-parent values across all years. The masking rate is ~22% for pregnant and ~24% for single-parent counts.

### Data Quality
- All 4 years delivered with consistent column structure
- No data gaps or missing years
- District count increases slightly each year (1087 to 1100), consistent with new PEIMS reporting
- 3 EML files are duplicates of the amendment letter under PIR #2502592 - not substantive responses

### Relation to PIR #2502592
This PIR covers the pre-pandemic baseline (2016-17 through 2019-20). PIR #2502592 covers 2020-21 through 2024-25, with a data source change (Fall to Summer submission in 2020-21). Together they provide the full 2016-17 through 2024-25 series.
